studies.title: Evaluation of "Invirto Therapy" for people with panic disorder: a randomized-controlled trial
studies.brief_summary:
The aim of the planned study is to investigate the efficacy of the app-based therapy "Invirto Therapy". The study will randomly assign 128 individuals diagnosed with panic disorder with a duration of at least one year to two groups (Invirto Therapy vs. Care as usual). All participants will be assessed at three points in time using self-assessment questionnaires (online assessment).
It is expected that the improvement in anxiety symptoms, depression symptoms, psychological flexibility, and quality of life will be larger in the Invirto Therapy group than in the control group.

studies.title: Research project to test the efficacy of an online follow-up program for patients with anxiety disorder using randomized controlled designs
studies.brief_summary: The aim of the planned study is to investigate the efficacy of an app-based program for the aftercare of psychotherapeutic treatment (Invirto aftercare). Within the framework of the research project, divided into three parallel studies, in total 414 patients each with diagnosed anxiety disorder and previous psychotherapy will be recruited and assessed three times using self-assessment questionnaires (online survey). It is expected that the intervention users will experience a greater decrease in specific anxiety symptoms including a lower relapse rate over the study period compared to the control group.
